title: Selling Without Stripe Using Crypto Payment Infrastructure: What Actually Works When You Are in a Restricted Country
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: You’ve built something. An ebook, a course, a set of presets, a beat – something people want. You’ve got buyers lined up, ready to pay… except the payment platforms everyone tells you to use just… don’t work for you. PayPal flags your country. Stripe isn’t even an option. Gumroad and Payhip require a bank account you can’t get, or a PayPal account that gets frozen the moment you start seeing real sales. It’s infuriating. You’re not doing anything wrong, you’re just born in the wrong place. I’ve talked to hundreds of creators in Nigeria, Pakistan, Venezuela, and everywhere in between who’ve hit this wall. They’re tired of being told ‘it’s complicated’ or ‘you need to find a friend in the US to handle payments.’ There’s a better way, and it doesn’t involve begging for permission.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When you’re looking for a way to get paid online, you’re not just comparing features like transaction fees or ease of use. You’re comparing two fundamentally different systems. Traditional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe are built on a centralized, permissioned infrastructure. They operate within the existing financial system, which means they’re subject to the rules and regulations of governments and banks. Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, is decentralized and permissionless. It doesn’t rely on intermediaries or gatekeepers. It’s a direct exchange of value between you and your customer, and no one can stop it. This isn’t about ‘crypto versus fiat’; it’s about access to a functioning financial system when you’ve been deliberately excluded.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Let’s be brutally honest: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip don’t *accidentally* exclude creators in certain countries. It’s a deliberate business decision. They assess ‘risk’ – and that risk assessment is heavily biased against emerging markets. High fraud rates (often unfairly attributed to entire countries), weak banking infrastructure, and complex KYC (Know Your Customer) regulations all contribute to these platforms simply refusing service or severely restricting accounts. Even if you manage to get approved, your account is constantly at risk of being frozen or terminated, leaving you scrambling to recover your funds. Gumroad’s reliance on PayPal is a particularly painful trap, as is Payhip’s limited payout options. Stripe’s availability is spotty at best, and often comes with exorbitant fees and lengthy approval processes. These aren’t technical limitations; they’re policy choices.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure bypasses the entire problem of geographic compliance. Using a payment processor like NOWPayments, which integrates seamlessly with tools like Unchained Commerce, you accept payments directly from your customers in Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT, or 300+ other cryptocurrencies. There’s no KYC required for your customers, and no need for you to have a bank account in a ‘whitelisted’ country. The transaction happens directly on the blockchain, and the funds are yours instantly. This isn’t about hoping a corporation will change its mind; it’s about building a system that doesn’t *need* their permission. It’s about financial sovereignty. The technology doesn’t care where you or your customers are located; it only cares about the validity of the transaction.
PayPal not being available in your country is not a you problem — it is a platform design decision that you are not obligated to accept.
The Real Cost Difference

People often assume crypto transactions are expensive. That’s often true on congested networks like Ethereum, but it’s not the whole story. NOWPayments offers low fees, and many cryptocurrencies (like TRX and SOL) have incredibly low transaction costs. More importantly, consider the *hidden* costs of using traditional platforms: the time wasted applying for accounts, the funds frozen in disputes, the lost sales due to restricted access, and the constant anxiety of potential account termination. Those costs add up quickly. With a one-time payment for a solution like the Unchained Commerce Full Stack Bundle, you gain lifetime access to a system that’s designed to work for you, without ongoing subscription fees or hidden charges. You own your infrastructure, and you control your revenue.
Which One to Use and Why

If you can seamlessly integrate with PayPal or Stripe, great. But if you’re in a restricted country, relying on those platforms is a recipe for frustration and lost income. It’s like building a house on sand. Crypto-native infrastructure isn’t a ‘backup plan’; it’s a superior solution for anyone who’s been excluded from the traditional financial system. It’s not about embracing crypto for the sake of it; it’s about using technology to solve a real problem. It’s about taking control of your business and getting paid for your work, regardless of where you’re located. It’s about building a sustainable business, not constantly fighting to stay afloat.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

The questions creators in restricted countries ask about payment platforms reveal exactly how the problem compounds over time. Every question about PayPal alternatives, Gumroad replacements, and Stripe workarounds comes from a creator who has already spent significant time trying to make the standard platforms work and found a wall at every turn. The answer to all of these questions is the same at the infrastructure level: the restriction is geographic and the solution is payment infrastructure that has no geographic layer. NOWPayments accepts 300-plus coins with no KYC. The Core Shop Engine automates delivery without depending on PayPal or Stripe. The Web3 Wallet Connect generates QR codes for every supported coin. No question about PayPal availability is relevant to a store running on this infrastructure because PayPal is not in the stack.
A platform that can decide your country does not qualify is a platform you are renting from on their terms, not infrastructure you own.
Related Topics Worth Reading

The searches that lead creators to this topic — PayPal alternatives, Gumroad replacements, Stripe workarounds for restricted countries — all converge on the same infrastructure answer. Every platform someone recommends as an alternative eventually reveals the same geographic limitation at the payout layer. The path that actually works is not finding a better platform within the same restricted system — it is building infrastructure outside the system entirely. Crypto-native payment rails exist for exactly this situation.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before promoting your store to an audience, work through these confirmations in order. The Core Shop Engine backend is running and connected to your NOWPayments account with at least five coins active and verified on mainnet — not sandbox. The Web3 Wallet Connect is generating correct QR codes for each enabled coin and the amounts match what NOWPayments expects. At least one complete end-to-end test transaction has been run on mainnet where real crypto moved, the payment confirmed, and the product arrived in the test inbox without any manual step. The Admin Dashboard is showing the test order with correct payment status, delivery status, and timestamp at each stage. The receipt email contains the transaction ID, the payment amount in both USD and the crypto coin used, and the product delivery confirmation. None of these confirmations can be skipped — each one catches a different class of failure before a real buyer encounters it.
Your first sale through crypto will feel different from your first sale through a platform workaround — it will feel like it actually belongs to you.
Full backend with bot engine, automated delivery, payment verification, and PDF receipts. No payment processor account required. Works everywhere.
